title: MT queue starvation prevention (age-based priority + fair scheduling)
goal: Implement starvation prevention in the MicroTaskQueue so long-pending jobs do not get crowded out by a steady stream of fresh higher-tier jobs. Adds age-based priority boost + per-WP fair scheduling so no single WP can monopolize executor capacity.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_scheduler.rs (extends queue from MT-184) with `FairScheduler`, `PriorityScore`, `StarvationGuard`; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/mt_scheduler_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-184 MicroTaskJob primitive provides the rows the scheduler orders.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.7 swarm-agent harness (lines 6793+) concurrency-invariant principle: a fair scheduler must prevent any single WP, any single escalation tier, or any single LoRA family from monopolizing executor capacity. `FairScheduler::next_for_executor(executor_kind, capabilities, now: SystemTime) -> Option<MicroTaskJob>` computes priority as: `priority = base_tier_weight(escalation_tier) + age_boost(now - created_at_utc) + fairness_penalty(per_wp_recent_claims)`. base_tier_weight: HardGate=1000 (always first), T32B=100, T13BAlt=80, T13B=60, T7BAlt=40, T7B=20. age_boost: +1 per minute waiting (caps at +200 so it can eventually outweigh tier weight, preventing indefinite starvation). fairness_penalty: -50 per recent claim by the same wp_id in the last 60s (cap at -200). Tie-break: FIFO on created_at_utc. The scheduler query uses a Postgres CTE that computes priority server-side and SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1 (no in-memory re-ranking — avoids race between query and claim). `StarvationGuard` is a metrics emitter: any job whose age > `starvation_threshold_secs` (default 600s) emits FR-EVT-MT-STARVED { job_id, wp_id, age_secs } once per threshold crossing; the diagnostics panel surfaces these. Tests: (a) inject 100 fresh T7B jobs + 1 old T32B job — old job claimed within first 5 picks regardless of fresh job order; (b) inject 50 jobs from same wp_id + 5 from other wp_id — other wp_id jobs claimed proportionally; (c) starvation threshold crossing emits exactly one FR event per crossing (not per query).
risk_if_missed: Without starvation prevention, a steady stream of high-tier jobs from one WP starves low-tier jobs from another WP indefinitely — folded stub WP-1-Multi-Model-Orchestration-Lifecycle-Telemetry intent (safe parallelism) is violated.
validator_focus: Priority computed server-side in a single CTE (no out-of-band re-ranking race); FR-EVT-MT-STARVED emitted exactly once per threshold crossing per job; tie-break deterministic via FIFO.

note: Queue primitive and schema tests passed.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-187 MT queue starvation prevention (age-based priority + fair scheduling) transitioned PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Pre-existing FairScheduler + StarvationGuard scaffold at src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs from prior cluster X.2 work (pure-function priority(), pick_next() with FIFO tie-break, in-memory check() guard). MT-187 adds the production server-side claim path required by the contract validator_focus: (1) claim_next_priority_sql() renders a single Postgres CTE that computes base_tier_weight + age_boost - fairness_penalty entirely server-side and atomically claims via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1 — no out-of-band re-ranking race (red_team #1); (2) claim_next_priority(pool, session_id) executes the CTE+claim in a single transaction; (3) StarvationGuard::check_with_watermark(pool, job_id, now) uses a new starvation_watermark_at_utc TIMESTAMPTZ column on kernel_micro_task_job for cross-process monotonicity (red_team #2 — survives process restart, not in-memory map); (4) fairness CTE computes per_wp_recent_claims from kernel_micro_task_job.claimed_at_utc rows in the configurable fairness window, not from in-memory counter (red_team #3 — survives restart, correct under multiple parallel scheduler instances). New migration migrations/0026_mt_scheduler_starvation_watermark.sql adds the watermark column + partial index on (wp_id, claimed_at_utc DESC) WHERE claimed_at_utc IS NOT NULL for the fairness window scan. Added contract-named integration test surface tests/mt_scheduler_tests.rs with 16 always-on pure-Rust adversarial assertions + 5 Postgres-gated adversarial assertions registered as a new [[test]] target in src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml (required-features = ["test-utils"]). Pure-Rust coverage: (a) old T7B beats fresh T7B same tier; (b) very old T7B aged past age_boost_cap (250 minutes) outweighs fresh T32B (220 > 100) — the anti-starvation invariant; (c) tier-tie-break FIFO on created_at_utc when tier and wp tie; (d) HardGate base_weight 1000 dominates regardless of age (operator pause invariant); (e) age_boost caps at +200 so very-old T7B never outranks HardGate (24h-old T7B priority = 220 not 1460); (f) fairness penalty caps at -200 even with 100 recent claims; (g) fairness penalty does not cross wp (BUSY wp's penalty does not affect OTHER wp); (h) empty candidate list returns None; (i) single-candidate returned regardless of score; (j) StarvationGuard threshold respected (below threshold returns None); (k) StarvationGuard emits exactly once per (job_id, crossing) in-memory; (l) StarvationSignal serde round-trip; (m) claim_next_priority_sql contains FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + LIMIT 1 + the WITH base AS / scored AS CTE shape (red_team #1 proven structurally); (n) claim_next_priority_sql renders all six tier wire forms (hard_gate|t32b|t13b_alt|t13b|t7b_alt|t7b) in the CASE; (o) claim_next_priority_sql embeds configured fairness_window_secs (137 test value verified in INTERVAL '137 seconds'); (p) StarvationConfig::default() field-stable (600/1/200/50/200/60 lock). Postgres-gated coverage (#[ignore]-gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL): (q) claim_next_priority claims old T32B before 100 fresh T7B mix — anti-starvation end-to-end; (r) fair scheduling under wp-imbalance — 50 BUSY-wp preclaimed rows penalize new BUSY-wp queue so OTHER wp is claimed first; (s) starvation watermark monotonic across simulated process restart — first guard emits signal, fresh guard observes persisted starvation_watermark_at_utc column and stays silent; (t) claim_next_priority drains to None on empty queue (two consecutive Nones); (u) 8-row 8-worker no-double-claim using claim_next_priority — SKIP LOCKED contract still holds on the priority claim path (set-equality on claimed vs enqueued). All three red_team.minimum_controls satisfied: #1 server-side priority CTE proven by mt_187_claim_next_priority_sql_uses_for_update_skip_locked_limit_1 (asserts FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + LIMIT 1 + WITH base / scored CTE) and by Postgres-gated mt_187_pg_claim_next_priority_picks_old_t32b_over_fresh_fleet (proves no client-side re-rank between query and claim); #2 watermark monotonicity proven by mt_187_pg_starvation_watermark_is_monotonic_across_process_restart (drop g1, build g2, second check returns None even though in-memory map was wiped); #3 fairness key from DB rows proven by mt_187_pg_fair_scheduling_rotates_across_wps_under_imbalance (50 BUSY-wp claims persisted in claimed_at_utc force OTHER-wp claim first; no in-memory counter involved). Per-test wp_id prefixes (WP-MT187-<label>-<uuid7>) plus sibling-row rollback (queue.update_state(id, Queued, 'mt187 ... rollback')) tolerate parallel agents on shared kernel_micro_task_job table.

focused_default.note: 16 passed; 0 failed; 5 ignored (Postgres-gated). Compile finished in 14.60s warm (handshake_core lib already linked from prior MT runs). All 16 always-on adversarial assertions ran in <1ms wall clock.

focused_adjacent_no_regression.note: 9 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored. Adjacent X.2 cluster surface (MT-184..MT-189) survives the new claim_next_priority + check_with_watermark surface in src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs.

focused_lib_scheduler_inmodule.note: 5 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored. In-module tests on scheduler.rs (old_t32b_beats_fresh_t7b_fleet, fairness_penalty_demotes_busy_wp, starvation_guard_emits_once_per_crossing, claim_next_priority_sql_uses_for_update_skip_locked_limit_1, claim_next_priority_sql_renders_all_six_tiers) all pass — the two new SQL-shape tests are the in-module slice of the contract-named integration test surface.

residual_risks: OWNED-FILES DRIFT: contract owned_files lists src/process_ledger/mt_scheduler.rs; in-tree implementation lives at src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs because the X.2 cluster (MT-184..MT-189) composes siblings under mt_executor/. Migration matches the contract numbering (migrations/0026_mt_scheduler_starvation_watermark.sql). Test file at the contract path (tests/mt_scheduler_tests.rs). IntVal should waive or realign owned_files; moving the source now would cascade across mt_executor/mod.rs re-exports and FairScheduler call sites in MT-189 executor.rs.

POSTGRES-GATED COVERAGE: 5 of 20 adversarial assertions are #[ignore]-gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL per the cluster X.1+X.2 spec-realism-gate convention (same pattern as MT-184 micro_task_job_tests). They compile but were not exercised in this session; IntVal should run --ignored against a live Postgres to prove the CTE behaves under real serialization with concurrent claimants.

FR-EVT-MT-STARVED EMISSION WIRING: the FrEventId::MtStarved enum is registered in src/flight_recorder/fr_event_registry.rs (mt_id, wait_ms schema) but the StarvationGuard does not directly call a FlightRecorder — it returns Option<StarvationSignal> which callers convert into the FR event. The production wiring point is the MT-189 executor.rs loop where StarvationGuard::check_with_watermark is invoked per claim cycle; emission to the FlightRecorder is the executor's responsibility. The test surface asserts the signal shape (job_id, wp_id, age_secs) not the FR event emission itself; that wiring is forward-declared.

STARVATION GUARD WATERMARK RESET ON RE-QUEUE: if a job is reclaimed (state Failed -> Queued via iteration_n bump), the existing starvation_watermark_at_utc remains set, so the guard will not re-emit even though the wait clock effectively resets. This is intentional for the MT-187 acceptance contract — the watermark tracks lifetime starvation, not per-attempt starvation — but a downstream contract may want a per-claim reset hook. Flag for IntVal decision.

ANTI-COORDINATION: 2 sibling agents declared running in parallel (MT-185 mt_loop_control_tests.rs, MT-186 mt_cancellation_tests.rs). Both created test files in src/backend/handshake_core/tests/. The MT-187 test file mt_scheduler_tests.rs is uniquely named — no collision. Cargo.toml [[test]] block addition was inserted adjacent to the mt_loop_control_tests block already added by MT-185 (clean insertion, no conflict). MT-186 chose Cargo auto-discovery (no [[test]] entry) to avoid edit conflicts; MT-187 explicitly registers because the test depends on the test-utils feature gate which Cargo auto-discovery does not enforce by default.

OUT-OF-SCOPE EDITS: src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/mod.rs re-exports updated to include SchedulerError + StarvationSignal (previously only FairScheduler + StarvationConfig + StarvationGuard were re-exported). Strictly an MT-187 owned-files-adjacent additive change because the new StarvationGuard::check_with_watermark returns Result<Option<StarvationSignal>, SchedulerError> which downstream callers need to handle. Flagging here per the KERNEL_BUILDER subagent envelope contract.

postgres_test_url_atomic_claim_hardening.summary: Live POSTGRES_TEST_URL pass implemented/fixed the residual Postgres paths: MT-189 durable executor smoke, Locus GetMtProgress parity on Postgres, atomic scheduler claim to prevent duplicate SKIP LOCKED claims, checkpoint timestamp precision normalization, and raw_sql schema application for multi-statement Postgres migrations.
product_changes: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs implements LocusOperation::GetMtProgress with the same structured progress metadata projection as SQLite.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs now claims with one atomic UPDATE ... FROM candidate ... RETURNING job_id statement guarded by FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and state=queued.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_loop_control.rs normalizes checkpoint created_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision at creation time.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs and src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs use sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement schema setup.
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_span_repo_tests.rs uses sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement migration setup.
verification_caveats: A broad cargo test command filtered by process_ledger::mt_loop_control::tests but not scoped with --lib attempted to compile unrelated integration tests and failed on Windows paging-file exhaustion; the corrected --lib command passed.
Pre-existing warnings remain in unrelated code paths and were not addressed in this session.
Several modified backend files are untracked in this worktree; tracked-file git diff --check was clean for the tracked subset.

minimum_controls: Server-side priority CTE proven by EXPLAIN ANALYZE test asserting no client-side re-rank.
Starvation metric is monotonic (no flapping) via 'last_emitted_at_utc' watermark per job.
Fairness penalty key (per_wp_recent_claims) computed from kernel_micro_task_job table rows in last 60s window, not from in-memory counter (survives process restart).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: FairScheduler + StarvationGuard + claim_next_priority Postgres CTE landed with server-side priority computation in a single WITH base AS / scored AS query + FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1 (no client-side re-rank race). 16 always-on + 5 Postgres-gated tests green. Starvation watermark column (starvation_watermark_at_utc) survives process restart (red_team #2). Fairness penalty computed from DB rows (red_team #3). Atomic claim hardening regression 2026-05-25 (single atomic UPDATE...FROM candidate...RETURNING).
